title: DNA biophysics predicts meiotic crossover hotspots independently of GC content

source.note: Maternal + paternal 1 Mb maps from Palsson et al. 2025 Nature 639:700-707. Columns: map (NCO rate), cMperMb (CO rate, Halldorsson 2019), DSB, oNCO. Staged from data/downloads/palsson2025/{maps.mat.tsv, maps.pat.tsv}. Not yet ingested to Fly Postgres per RESULT.md — fixture pins the local RDS.
25 biophysical tracks at 1 kb resolution from Polymer_Evolution phases 1 (8 tracks), 2 (7), 3 (6), 3.5 (4). Registry in internal/InSilico/recombination\ hotspots/R/00_config.R.

operations.note: Mean of each biophysical track within each 1 Mb autosomal window.
dipole_density is perfectly collinear with gc_content and is dropped prior to modeling (R/07_random_forest_a6.R:30-31). Result: 24 biophysics features retained (including gc_content).
Target 'hot' = top 25% CO rate. Threshold is data-derived (co_q75), first-class as a DataDerivedConstant so it is hashable and recomputable.

statistics.name: auroc_mean_cv
statistics.note: Mean AUROC over 5 chromosome-held-out folds. GC-only baseline.

statistics.name: auroc_mean_cv
statistics.note: All biophysics with gc_content removed. M2 > M1 — removing GC improves prediction.

statistics.name: delta_auroc_vs_gc_only
statistics.note: Derived: s_auroc_m1 − s_auroc_m0. Threshold for STRONG positive was 0.05.

statistics.name: delta_auroc_vs_gc_only
statistics.note: Derived: s_auroc_m2 − s_auroc_m0. GC-free biophysics outperforms GC-only by 12.8 AUROC points.

statistics.name: top_importance_feature
statistics.note: Rank-1 permutation importance in M1. Layer-2 (Polymer_Evolution phase 3) nucleosome-lattice property, not a composition descriptor. gc_content ranks 8th. Full ranked list in results/a6_feature_importance.csv.

inference.justification: Three conjuncts license the conclusion: (1) ΔAUROC(M1 − M0) > 0.05 passes the pre-registered STRONG-positive threshold in the experiment plan (RESULT.md A6); (2) M2 ≥ M1 shows removing gc_content does not harm prediction, establishing GC-independence beyond mere partial-correlation survival; (3) M2 > 0.8 ensures absolute performance is substantively predictive.
inference.failure_mode: If s_auroc_m2 dropped near s_auroc_m0 (~0.70) after removing gc_content, the signal would collapse to a GC proxy and the conclusion would not be licensed. Current gap (s_auroc_m2 − s_auroc_m0 = 0.128) is load-bearing.

conclusion.assertion: At 1 Mb resolution on autosomes, DNA biophysical properties predict meiotic crossover hotspots (top-quartile cM/Mb) independently of GC content; Layer-2 nucleosome-lattice properties (quantization_map) dominate feature importance, with gc_content ranked 8th.

scope.scale_note: Claim is explicitly at 1 Mb. Sub-1-Mb generalization (A5 on 28,660 oNCO events; DMC1 peaks at ~300 bp) is a separate claim object, deferred to future fixtures. Noted in RESULT.md §Bolstering as the single most impactful follow-up.

confidence.note: Per-statistic SDs reported in results/a6_rf_results.csv; composite CI attached via composite_confidence below.

composite_confidence.note: Not yet computed. Intended procedure: stratified-by-chromosome bootstrap of the 5-fold CV pipeline; report the fraction of resamples for which all three inference conjuncts hold.

external_assumptions.statement: Halldorsson et al. 2019 (cMperMb) is a reliable 1 Mb estimate of the true meiotic crossover rate.
Palsson et al. 2025 maternal and paternal maps are well-calibrated at 1 Mb for CO and NCO rates.
Autosome-only analysis is representative of meiotic crossover biology for the scope of this claim; chrX/chrY behavior is not addressed.
ΔG37 values computed at 37 °C are acceptable approximations for the ~33–34 °C testicular meiotic context; the ~2–3% free-energy shift does not invalidate relative comparisons between windows.

Removing dipole_density (perfectly collinear with gc_content) does not leak GC information into M2; any residual GC signal in the retained 23 tracks is uncontrolled but expected to be small given the explicit GC–Arrangement decomposition in CLAUDE.md.
